content:
# Matthew Chapter 20 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_020_page_001.jpg

1. "For the kingdom of the heavens is like a man, a householder, who went out early in the morning to hire workers for the vineyard of him.

2. And having agreed with the workers for a denarius the day, he sent them into the vineyard of him.

3. And having gone out about the third hour, he saw others standing in the marketplace idle,

4. and to those he said, 'You also go into the vineyard, and whatever is right I will give to you.'

5. And they went. Having gone out again about the sixth and ninth hour, he did likewise.

6. And about the eleventh [hour] having gone out, he found others standing, and says to them, 'Why have you stood here the whole day idle?'

7. They say to him, 'Because no one hired us.' He says to them, 'You also go into the vineyard.'

8. And evening having come, the lord of the vineyard says to the manager of him, 'Call the workers and pay to them the wage, beginning from the last to the first.'

9. And having come, the [ones hired] about the eleventh hour received each a denarius.

10. And having come, the first supposed that they would receive more; and they also received each a denarius.

11. And having received [it], they were grumbling against the householder,

12. saying, 'These last worked one hour, and you made them equal to us, the [ones] having borne the burden of the day and the scorching-heat.'

13. And answering, he said to one of them, 'Friend, I do not wrong you. Did you not agree with me for a denarius?

14. Take what is yours and go. But I wish to give to this last as also to you.

15. Is it not lawful for me to do what I wish with what is mine? Or is the eye of you evil because I am good?'

16. So the last will be first, and the first last."

17. And going up to Hierosolyma, the Yeshua took the twelve disciples privately, and on the road he said to them,

18. "Behold, we go up to Hierosolyma, and the Son of the Man will be delivered to the chief priests and scribes, and they will condemn him to death,

19. and they will deliver him to the nations for mocking and flogging and crucifying, and on the third day he will be raised."

20. Then came to him the mother of the sons of Zebedaios with the sons of her, worshiping and asking something from him.

21. And he said to her, "What do you wish?" She says to him, "Say that these two sons of me may sit one at the right of you and one at the left in the kingdom of you."

22. And answering, the Yeshua said, "You do not know what you ask. Are you able to drink the cup which I am about to drink?" They say to him, "We are able."

23. He says to them, "The cup of me indeed you will drink; but to sit at the right of me and at the left, this is not mine to give, but [is for those] for whom it has been prepared by the Father of me."

24. And having heard, the ten were indignant concerning the two brothers.

25. And the Yeshua, having summoned them, said, "You know that the rulers of the nations lord-it-over them, and the great [ones] exercise-authority over them.

26. It will not be so among you; but whoever wishes to become great among you will be the servant of you,

27. and whoever wishes to be first among you will be the slave of you —

28. just as the Son of the Man came not to be served but to serve, and to give the soul of him [as] a ransom for many."

29. And of them going out from Iericho, a great crowd followed him.

30. And behold, two blind [men] sitting beside the road, having heard that the Yeshua passes by, cried out, saying, "Have mercy on us, Lord, Son of David!"

31. And the crowd rebuked them that they should be quiet; but they cried out more, saying, "Have mercy on us, Lord, Son of David!"

32. And having stopped, the Yeshua called them and said, "What do you wish I should do for you?"

33. They say to him, "Lord, that the eyes of us may be opened."

34. And having been moved-with-compassion, the Yeshua touched the eyes of them, and immediately they received-sight and followed him.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **δηνάριον (vv.2, 9, 10, 13):** "Denarius" — a day's wage for a common laborer. The parable's point is not economics but grace: God's generosity does not follow the logic of human merit.

2. **v.15 — "Is your eye evil?":** The "evil eye" (ὀφθαλμὸς πονηρός) is a Semitic idiom for envy or stinginess. The workers are envious because the householder is generous.

3. **λύτρον (v.28):** "Ransom" — the price paid to release a captive or slave. Jesus' life is the ransom "for many" (ἀντὶ πολλῶν), echoing Isaiah 53:11-12 (the Suffering Servant who bears the sins of many).

4. **v.19 — Third passion prediction:** The most detailed yet: mockery, flogging, crucifixion. Each successive prediction adds specificity, moving toward the cross with increasing clarity.

— EMET, AI Translator
